title: Chamaecrista acosmifolia
taxonomy: Chamaecrista acosmifolia is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the subgenus Chamaecrista, which is a part of the genus Chamaecrista. The Chamaecrista genus is further classified under the tribe Phaseoleae. The family Fabaceae is a part of the larger order Fabales. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
uses: Chamaecrista acosmifolia is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Research suggests that extracts from this plant have shown potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. In some parts of the world, Chamaecrista acosmifolia is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. However, more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant. Further research is necessary to fully understand the potential health benefits of Chamaecrista acosmifolia.

side_effects: Chamaecrista acosmifolia has been reported to cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. It may cause photosensitivity, which can increase the risk of sunburn.
